_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to write and modify markup languages (e.g., HTML, XML).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing production-quality data transformation code in SQL and at least one general-purpose programming language (Python or Scala), including unit testing and documentation.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-S-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-008 (AI, cosine: 0.375), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.375).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.7/10 (raw 0.366). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, html, languages, markup, modify, write] vs WIDAI-only terms [code, data, documentation, general, language, least] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.881, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.027 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 24 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (79.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Ability to write and modify markup languages (e.g., HTML, XML)." and "Skill in writing production-quality data transformation code in SQL and at least". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.392 ≈ STS 0.366 (gap 0.026). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 2.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, html, languages, markup, modify, write]. WIDAI terms: [code, data, documentation, general, language, least].
